question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / IRepayCallback.onRepay] Can an unprivileged borrower acting as repay callback receiver, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].debt > 0 and marketState[id].withdrawable is non-zero, trigger repay(market, units, onBehalf, callback, data) which reduces debt and increases withdrawable BEFORE calling onRepay and BEFORE safeTransferFrom pulls loan token, then inside onRepay reenter withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) to drain the newly withdrawable units, violating the invariant that safeTransferFrom must complete before withdrawable assets are accessible, causing scoped impact: attacker withdraws loan tokens without ever transferring repayment funds, draining protocol balance? Proof idea: deploy MaliciousRepayCallback that calls withdraw() inside onRepay; assert Midnight.loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) decreases by units while no safeTransferFrom from payer succeeded.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator acting as liquidate callback receiver, under preconditions where borrower is unhealthy and seizedAssets > 0 and repaidUnits > 0, trigger liquidate() which calls safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) and increases marketState[id].withdrawable by repaidUnits BEFORE calling onLiquidate and BEFORE safeTransferFrom pulls loan token, then inside onLiquidate reenter withdraw(market, repaidUnits, creditHolder, receiver) to drain the withdrawable increase, violating the invariant that loan token repayment must precede any withdrawable credit redemption, causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral and drains withdrawable credit without paying repaidUnits in loan token? Proof idea: deploy MaliciousLiquidateCallback that calls withdraw() inside onLiquidate; assert loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) decreases without a corresponding safeTransferFrom completing.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / ISellCallback.onSell] Can an unprivileged taker acting as sellerCallback receiver, under preconditions where offer.buy == false and sellerDebtIncrease > 0 and the seller has collateral near the health threshold, trigger take() which completes both safeTransferFrom calls (line 455-456) and then calls onSell BEFORE the health check at line 476, then inside onSell reenter withdrawCollateral() to remove collateral from the seller's position, violating the invariant that the seller must remain healthy after take, causing scoped impact: seller's position becomes undercollateralized immediately after take with no revert, creating bad debt? Proof idea: deploy MaliciousSellCallback that calls withdrawCollateral() inside onSell; assert isHealthy(market, id, seller) == false after take() returns without reverting.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / IBuyCallback.onBuy] Can an unprivileged maker acting as buyerCallback, under preconditions where offer.buy == true and buyerCreditIncrease > 0 and consumed[maker][group] is already updated before onBuy fires, trigger take() which updates all position state and consumed state then calls onBuy, then inside onBuy reenter take() with the same offer struct, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] must prevent overfill, causing scoped impact: offer is filled beyond maxAssets or maxUnits because the inner take() sees the already-incremented consumed value and the outer take() does not re-check? Proof idea: fuzz units such that two nested take() calls together exceed offer.maxAssets; assert consumed[maker][group] > offer.maxAssets after both calls.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / safeTransferFrom (ERC777 loanToken hook)] Can an unprivileged taker using an ERC777-compatible loan token with a tokensReceived hook on address(Midnight), under preconditions where offer.buy == true and buyerCallback == address(0), trigger take() which calls safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) at line 455, causing the ERC777 hook to fire on address(Midnight) mid-transfer, then inside the hook reenter repay() or withdraw() while position state is already updated but sellerCallback has not yet been called, violating the invariant that no external call should observe partial take state, causing scoped impact: accounting desync between credit/debt units and actual token balances? Proof idea: deploy ERC777LoanToken with hook that calls repay(); assert position[id][seller].debt and marketState[id].withdrawable are inconsistent after take() completes.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / safeTransferFrom (ERC777 loanToken hook on receiver)] Can an unprivileged taker using an ERC777 loan token where receiver is a contract with tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where offer.buy == true and sellerAssets > 0, trigger take() which calls safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) at line 456, causing the ERC777 hook on receiver to fire before sellerCallback is invoked, then inside the hook reenter take() or liquidate() while the seller's liquidation lock is set but sellerCallback has not yet been called, violating the invariant that sellerCallback must complete before any external state observation, causing scoped impact: state corruption allowing double-credit or double-debt creation? Proof idea: deploy ERC777ReceiverHook that reenters take(); assert totalUnits and position credit/debt are consistent after both calls.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: withdraw / safeTransfer (ERC777 loanToken hook)] Can an unprivileged lender using an ERC777 loan token where receiver has a tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].credit >= units and marketState[id].withdrawable >= units, trigger withdraw() which reduces credit and withdrawable BEFORE calling safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) at line 499, causing the ERC777 hook to fire on receiver mid-transfer, then inside the hook reenter withdraw() again with the same or different units, violating the invariant that credit and withdrawable must not go below zero, causing scoped impact: double-withdrawal draining more loan tokens than the lender's credit entitles them to? Proof idea: deploy ERC777ReceiverHook that calls withdraw() inside tokensReceived; assert loanToken.balanceOf(receiver) > initial credit units after both withdrawals.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: withdrawCollateral / safeTransfer (ERC777 collateral hook)] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777 collateral token where receiver has a tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].collateral[index] >= assets and isHealthy passes after reduction, trigger withdrawCollateral() which reduces collateral and clears bitmap BEFORE calling safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) at line 572, causing the ERC777 hook to fire, then inside the hook reenter withdrawCollateral() again for the same collateral index, violating the invariant that collateral[index] must not underflow and bitmap must stay consistent, causing scoped impact: borrower withdraws more collateral than deposited, leaving position undercollateralized with no revert? Proof idea: deploy ERC777CollateralToken; assert position[id][borrower].collateral[index] underflows or isHealthy returns false after double withdrawal.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / safeTransfer (ERC777 collateral hook on receiver)] Can an unprivileged liquidator using an ERC777 collateral token where receiver has a tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where borrower is unhealthy and seizedAssets > 0, trigger liquidate() which reduces position.collateral[index] and clears bitmap BEFORE calling safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) at line 696, causing the ERC777 hook to fire, then inside the hook reenter liquidate() on the same borrower with seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 to realize bad debt again, violating the invariant that bad debt must be realized exactly once per liquidation, causing scoped impact: lossFactor is updated twice for the same bad debt, over-slashing lender credit? Proof idea: deploy ERC777CollateralReceiver that calls liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) inside hook; assert marketState[id].lossFactor is updated more than once for the same badDebt amount.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: supplyCollateral / safeTransferFrom (ERC777 collateral hook on Midnight)] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777 collateral token with a tokensReceived hook on address(Midnight), under preconditions where oldCollateral == 0 so bitmap is set before safeTransferFrom fires, trigger supplyCollateral() which sets collateralBitmap and increments collateral[index] BEFORE calling safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) at line 545, causing the ERC777 hook to fire on Midnight, then inside the hook reenter withdrawCollateral() to withdraw the collateral before the transfer completes, violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual token balance, causing scoped impact: borrower activates a collateral slot and immediately withdraws it without ever transferring tokens, inflating collateral valuation? Proof idea: deploy ERC777CollateralToken; assert collateral[index] > 0 and collateralToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) == 0 after the attack.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / safeTransferFrom (ERC777 loanToken hook on Midnight)] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777 loan token with a tokensReceived hook on address(Midnight), under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].debt >= units and callback == address(0), trigger repay() which reduces debt and increases withdrawable BEFORE calling safeTransferFrom(loanToken, msg.sender, address(this), units) at line 520, causing the ERC777 hook to fire on Midnight mid-transfer, then inside the hook reenter take() to increase debt again before repay's safeTransferFrom completes, violating the invariant that debt reduction must be atomic with token receipt, causing scoped impact: borrower reduces debt in accounting without paying, then re-borrows, creating unbacked debt? Proof idea: deploy ERC777LoanToken; assert position[id][borrower].debt and loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) are inconsistent after attack.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / fee-on-transfer loanToken] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token, under preconditions where the token deducts a percentage fee on every transfer, trigger take() which calls safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) at line 455 and safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) at line 456, but Midnight receives less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets due to the fee, while claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is incremented by the full buyerAssets - sellerAssets at line 418, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must not exceed actual token balance attributable to fees, causing scoped impact: feeClaimer can claim more settlement fee than the protocol actually holds, draining lender withdrawable funds? Proof idea: fuzz fee percentage; assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] > loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) - marketState[id].withdrawable after take().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / fee-on-transfer loanToken] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token, under preconditions where the token deducts a fee on transferFrom, trigger repay() which increases marketState[id].withdrawable by units at line 509 but safeTransferFrom at line 520 delivers only units * (1 - fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that withdrawable must not exceed actual redeemable loan token balance, causing scoped impact: lenders can withdraw more loan tokens than the protocol holds, leading to insolvency for the last withdrawers? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate in [1, 100] bps; assert sum of all position credits <= loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) after repay().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: supplyCollateral / fee-on-transfer collateralToken] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token, under preconditions where the token deducts a fee on transferFrom, trigger supplyCollateral() which sets position[id][onBehalf].collateral[index] = oldCollateral + assets at line 533 but safeTransferFrom at line 545 delivers only assets * (1 - fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual token balance, causing scoped impact: borrower's collateral valuation is inflated, allowing them to borrow more than their actual collateral supports, creating bad debt? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate; assert position[id][borrower].collateral[index] > collateralToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) after supplyCollateral(); then verify isHealthy returns true for a position that would be unhealthy with actual balances.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / fee-on-transfer loanToken] Can an unprivileged liquidator using a fee-on-transfer loan token, under preconditions where repaidUnits > 0 and the token deducts a fee on transferFrom, trigger liquidate() which increases marketState[id].withdrawable by repaidUnits at line 675 but safeTransferFrom at line 717 delivers only repaidUnits * (1 - fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that withdrawable must not exceed actual token balance, causing scoped impact: lenders can withdraw more than the protocol holds after liquidation, causing insolvency? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate; assert marketState[id].withdrawable > loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) after liquidate().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: flashLoan / fee-on-transfer token] Can an unprivileged flash loan user using a fee-on-transfer token, under preconditions where the token deducts a fee on both transfer and transferFrom, trigger flashLoan() which calls safeTransfer(tokens[i], callback, assets[i]) at line 743 delivering assets[i] * (1 - fee_rate) to callback, then calls safeTransferFrom(tokens[i], callback, address(this), assets[i]) at line 750 pulling assets[i] from callback but Midnight receives only assets[i] * (1 - fee_rate), violating the invariant that flashLoan must be net-zero for the protocol, causing scoped impact: protocol loses fee_rate * assets[i] tokens per flash loan, draining token balance over repeated calls? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate and assets[i]; assert loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) decreases by fee_rate * assets[i] per flashLoan call.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / payer confusion with buyerCallback] Can an unprivileged taker setting takerCallback as a malicious contract, under preconditions where offer.buy == false so takerCallback becomes buyerCallback (line 420) and payer = buyerCallback (line 422), trigger take() which calls onBuy on the malicious buyerCallback, then calls safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) and safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, receiver, sellerAssets), where the malicious buyerCallback inside onBuy drains its own loan token balance to zero before safeTransferFrom fires, causing safeTransferFrom to revert, violating the invariant that take() must atomically settle or fully revert, causing scoped impact: state is updated (consumed, positions) but the transaction reverts, leaving consumed incremented without a successful fill? Proof idea: verify that consumed[maker][group] is NOT persisted if safeTransferFrom reverts (Solidity reverts all state); confirm no partial-state window exists.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / IBuyCallback.onBuy reenters repay()] Can an unprivileged maker whose offer.callback is a malicious contract, under preconditions where offer.buy == true and the seller has existing debt, trigger take() which updates seller's debt position and sets liquidation lock, then calls onBuy on the maker's callback, then inside onBuy calls repay(market, sellerDebt, seller, address(0), '') to reduce seller's debt to zero, then the outer take() calls safeTransferFrom pulling funds from the callback, violating the invariant that the seller's debt state observed at health check must reflect the state after take() not after an intermediate repay(), causing scoped impact: seller's health check passes trivially because debt was repaid inside callback, masking an otherwise unhealthy position? Proof idea: assert that after take() + onBuy-repay(), seller.debt == 0 and isHealthy trivially passes; verify this cannot be used to bypass the SellerIsLiquidatable check.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / ISellCallback.onSell reenters take()] Can an unprivileged taker whose takerCallback is a malicious contract, under preconditions where offer.buy == true and sellerCallback = takerCallback, trigger take() which completes safeTransferFrom calls then calls onSell, then inside onSell calls take() again with a new sell offer for the same seller, increasing seller's debt further, violating the invariant that the outer take()'s health check at line 476 must evaluate the seller's final debt state, causing scoped impact: seller's debt is increased inside sellerCallback after the outer take's accounting but before the outer health check, and the inner take's health check passes because it runs first, while the outer health check may also pass due to stale state? Proof idea: fuzz units for inner and outer take; assert seller.debt after both takes does not exceed maxDebt.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / ISellCallback.onSell reenters liquidate()] Can an unprivileged taker whose takerCallback is a malicious liquidator contract, under preconditions where offer.buy == true and a third-party borrower is unhealthy, trigger take() which sets the liquidation lock for the seller (not the third-party borrower), then calls onSell, then inside onSell calls liquidate() on the unhealthy third-party borrower, violating the invariant that liquidation lock only protects the current take's seller, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a different borrower inside sellerCallback corrupts marketState (withdrawable, lossFactor, totalUnits) while the outer take's health check and accounting are still in progress? Proof idea: assert marketState[id].withdrawable and totalUnits are consistent after nested take+liquidate; check for lossFactor double-update.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / IRepayCallback.onRepay reenters take()] Can an unprivileged borrower acting as repay callback, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].debt > 0 and a sell offer exists for the same market, trigger repay() which reduces debt and increases withdrawable BEFORE onRepay fires, then inside onRepay calls take() with a sell offer to increase debt again, violating the invariant that repay must atomically reduce debt and receive loan token before any new debt can be created, causing scoped impact: borrower repays debt in accounting, borrows again inside callback, then repay's safeTransferFrom pulls funds for the original repay amount while the new debt is unbacked? Proof idea: assert position[id][borrower].debt after repay+take-inside-callback equals original_debt - repaid + new_borrow; verify loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) covers all outstanding withdrawable.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate reenters liquidate()] Can an unprivileged liquidator acting as liquidate callback, under preconditions where borrower has multiple collateral assets and is unhealthy, trigger liquidate() on collateral index 0 which reduces debt and sends collateral to receiver BEFORE onLiquidate fires, then inside onLiquidate calls liquidate() again on collateral index 1 of the same borrower, violating the invariant that bad debt must be realized exactly once per liquidation event, causing scoped impact: badDebt is computed from the already-reduced debt in the inner call, causing lossFactor to be updated twice and over-slashing lender credit? Proof idea: assert marketState[id].lossFactor after nested liquidations equals the value expected from a single combined liquidation; check for double bad-debt realization.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate reenters withdraw()] Can an unprivileged liquidator acting as liquidate callback, under preconditions where marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by repaidUnits at line 675 before onLiquidate fires and a lender has credit >= repaidUnits, trigger liquidate() then inside onLiquidate call withdraw(market, repaidUnits, lender, attacker) to drain the withdrawable increase before safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) at line 717 pulls the loan token, violating the invariant that withdrawable must be backed by actual token balance, causing scoped impact: lender withdraws loan tokens that have not yet been deposited by the liquidator, draining protocol balance? Proof idea: assert loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) does not go negative; check that withdraw inside onLiquidate succeeds before safeTransferFrom completes.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: claimSettlementFee / safeTransfer (ERC777 hook)] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer using an ERC777 loan token where receiver has a tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where claimableSettlementFee[token] >= amount, trigger claimSettlementFee() which reduces claimableSettlementFee[token] by amount BEFORE calling safeTransfer(token, receiver, amount) at line 309, causing the ERC777 hook to fire on receiver, then inside the hook reenter claimSettlementFee() again with the same or different amount, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must not be claimed more than once, causing scoped impact: feeClaimer double-claims settlement fees, draining loan token balance beyond what was accrued? Proof idea: deploy ERC777ReceiverHook that calls claimSettlementFee() inside tokensReceived; assert claimableSettlementFee[token] underflows or loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) goes below expected floor.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: claimContinuousFee / safeTransfer (ERC777 hook)] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer using an ERC777 loan token where receiver has a tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit >= amount, trigger claimContinuousFee() which reduces continuousFeeCredit, totalUnits, and withdrawable BEFORE calling safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, amount) at line 324, causing the ERC777 hook to fire, then inside the hook reenter claimContinuousFee() again, violating the invariant that continuousFeeCredit must not be claimed more than once, causing scoped impact: feeClaimer drains more loan tokens than accrued as continuous fees, reducing lender withdrawable below their credit entitlement? Proof idea: deploy ERC777ReceiverHook; assert marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit does not underflow and loanToken.balanceOf(address(Midnight)) >= marketState[id].withdrawable after attack.
